blocks.body: Past Modals help you make deductions about the past and talk about regret, criticism, or possible past results.

{"level":"B1-B2","intro":"In this topic you will see how must have, might have, could have, can't have, and should have help you talk about past deduction, regret, criticism, and a possible past result.","rules":[{"label":"PAST DEDUCTION","color":"emerald","text":"Must have = I am almost sure this happened in the past.","example":"She must have forgotten."},{"label":"PAST POSSIBILITY","color":"blue","text":"Might have / could have = this was possible, but not certain.","example":"He might have taken the wrong train."},{"label":"REGRET / CRITICISM","color":"amber","text":"Should have / shouldn't have = it was the better or worse choice in the past.","example":"You should have called me."}]}

{"title":"1. The main pattern","intro":"For past modals, you very often need the structure modal + have + V3.","items":[{"label":"Strong deduction","title":"must have + V3","subtitle":"must have left / must have forgotten"},{"label":"Possible past","title":"might have / could have + V3","subtitle":"might have missed / could have taken"},{"label":"Impossible past","title":"can't have + V3","subtitle":"can't have gone / can't have seen"},{"label":"Regret / criticism","title":"should have / shouldn't have + V3","subtitle":"should have called / shouldn't have said"}]}

{"title":"2. Main uses","sections":[{"label":"Must have = strong conclusion about the past","color":"emerald","description":"Use must have when the evidence now strongly suggests that something happened earlier.","examples":[{"en":"She isn't here. She must have gone home.","ua":"She isn't here. She must have gone home."},{"en":"They look exhausted. They must have worked all night.","ua":"They look exhausted. They must have worked all night."},{"en":"The cake is gone. Someone must have eaten it.","ua":"The cake is gone. Someone must have eaten it."}]},{"label":"Might have / could have = possible past event","color":"blue","description":"These forms show that something was possible or may have happened, but you are not certain.","examples":[{"en":"He might have missed the email.","ua":"He might have missed the email."},{"en":"She could have taken a taxi.","ua":"She could have taken a taxi."},{"en":"We might have met before.","ua":"We might have met before."}],"note":"Could have can also talk about an unrealized possibility: You could have told me. Context decides the meaning."},{"label":"Should have = regret or criticism","color":"amber","description":"Should have and shouldn't have help you say that there was a better or worse choice in the past.","examples":[{"en":"I should have studied more for the exam.","ua":"I should have studied more for the exam."},{"en":"You shouldn't have said that.","ua":"You shouldn't have said that."},{"en":"They should have booked the tickets earlier.","ua":"They should have booked the tickets earlier."}],"note":"This is a common pattern for regret and criticism."}]}

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using had instead of have after the modal.","wrong":"She must had left early.","right":"✅ She must have left early.","hint":"The pattern is fixed: modal + have + V3."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using the second form instead of V3.","wrong":"He can't have went home.","right":"✅ He can't have gone home.","hint":"After have, you need the past participle, not Past Simple."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Writing should of instead of should have.","wrong":"I should of told you.","right":"✅ I should have told you.","hint":"It may sound similar in fast speech, but the correct word is have."}]}

{"title":"4. Quick rule","items":["Must have + V3 = strong conclusion about the past.","Might have / could have + V3 = possible past event or result.","Can't have + V3 = the past situation is logically impossible.","Should have / shouldn't have + V3 = it was a better or worse choice.","Remember the pattern: modal + have + V3."]}
